Operation 'scrapeUrl' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The original summary 'Scrape a URL' is too generic and doesn't convey the operation's advanced capabilities. The description, while comprehensive about features, lacks clarity on: (1) what parameters are required vs optional, (2) what the response contains beyond HTML, (3) authentication requirements, and (4) how the automatic retry and CAPTCHA handling work. The improved summary emphasizes the core action with key differentiators. The enhanced description adds context about authentication, clarifies the automatic handling of retries/CAPTCHA (mentioned in API overview), specifies what is returned, and better positions the operation for AI agent decision-making about when to use it.

data.current_description: Fetch the raw HTML content of any URL. Supports JavaScript rendering, custom headers, geographic targeting, device type emulation, premium residential proxies, and parser addons. Each request is retried until completed (up to 60 seconds). Limit is 2MB per request.
data.summary_suggestion: Fetch HTML content from any URL with advanced options
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the raw HTML content from a specified URL with support for JavaScript rendering, custom headers, geographic location targeting, device type emulation, premium residential proxies, and parser addons. The operation automatically handles retries and CAPTCHA resolution, with a maximum timeout of 60 seconds and 2MB response limit per request. Returns the scraped HTML content and metadata. Requires API key authentication via query parameter.
data.reasoning: The original summary 'Scrape a URL' is too generic and doesn't convey the operation's advanced capabilities. The description, while comprehensive about features, lacks clarity on: (1) what parameters are required vs optional, (2) what the response contains beyond HTML, (3) authentication requirements, and (4) how the automatic retry and CAPTCHA handling work. The improved summary emphasizes the core action with key differentiators. The enhanced description adds context about authentication, clarifies the automatic handling of retries/CAPTCHA (mentioned in API overview), specifies what is returned, and better positions the operation for AI agent decision-making about when to use it.
